what_it_does: A small shared stylesheet providing the common frame for design-system preview cards. It imports the colour/type tokens, resets html/body margins, sets body font/colour/background from tokens with antialiasing, and defines a handful of layout/utility classes: .card (padded full-size container), .card-row (flex wrap row), .card-grid (grid), .label (uppercase muted caption), and .mono (mono secondary text).
observations: @import of ../colors_and_type.css pulls in the token definitions this file consumes.
Body uses var(--font-body), var(--fg-primary), var(--bg-canvas) plus -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased.
.card-row is a flex row (center-aligned, gap 16px, wrap); .card-grid is a grid with gap 16px (no template).
.label is 11px 600 uppercase with 0.08em letter-spacing in var(--fg-muted).
Contains a few hardcoded px values (24/28/16/11/12px, 0.08em) rather than spacing tokens.
standouts: Tiny shared frame — only fonts/colours come from tokens; spacing/sizes are literal px.
conventions: Follows the token-reference convention for colour and font (var(--font-body)/--fg-*/--bg-canvas).
Deviates from the 'no raw px in consumers' rule by hardcoding spacing/size literals (padding, gaps, font-sizes).
Uses --fg-*/--bg-canvas role names rather than the --ink/--zone-* names seen in slide-archetypes.html, suggesting two token namings in play.
concerns: Hardcoded px (24/28/16/11/12, 0.08em) are off-token literals that the system's check.py/design-lint would flag.
Token names here (--fg-primary/--fg-secondary/--fg-muted/--bg-canvas) differ from --ink/--zone-ground used elsewhere in the corpus — a possible naming inconsistency between preview and core.
notes: The shared preview-card frame stylesheet: imports the colour/type tokens and defines .card/.card-row/.card-grid/.label/.mono, with colours tokenised but spacing/sizes hardcoded.
questions: Are --fg-primary/--fg-secondary/--fg-muted/--bg-canvas the preview-only token set, or aliases of the core --ink/--zone-* tokens?
Should the px spacing/font-size literals here be replaced with --d-*/--fs-* tokens to satisfy the anti-drift rule?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header says it is the shared frame for design-system cards and the file provides exactly that minimal frame and utilities.
apparent_themes: Shared frame / reuse over per-card CSS
intention_signals: Give every preview card one consistent, token-driven frame and a small set of reusable layout/text utilities so cards don't each re-style their shell.
contribution: Contributes the shared preview-card shell and its small utility class vocabulary (.card/.card-row/.card-grid/.label/.mono) that a fused system would keep for card framing.
summary_for_embedding: A small shared CSS file framing design-system preview cards: imports colour/type tokens, resets body, and defines .card/.card-row/.card-grid/.label/.mono utilities.
intention_for_embedding: To give all preview cards one consistent token-driven shell and reusable layout/text utilities.
